evidence.sourceText: While the intake packet was submitted to Quantum Residential staff at the Hillside Manor leasing office, it was not provided to The Housing Authority of Clackamas County. ... Ask Quantum Residential staff at the Hillside Manor leasing office to send the previously submitted intake packet directly to me, along with updated income verification if there have been any changes. / The following documents were received from the Hillside Manor office staff and are attached for reference: cortez 1.pdf, cortez 2.pdf, cortez 3.pdf, cortez 4.pdf.

evidence.sourceText: 90-day notice of displacement — Hillside Park Apartments

Household member with severe stair and mobility limitations. Mobility scooter cannot be used on third floor. HACC proposed third-floor unit as replacement housing. No accessible ground-floor alternative provided.
evidence.documentLabel: Exhibit I — Accommodation request records (mobility scooter / third-floor incompatibility)

evidence.sourceText: After Section 18 disposition approval and receipt of Tenant Protection Vouchers, residents can begin the relocation process with guidance from HACC and its relocation consultants. Residents will be given the option to move into a new unit at Hillside Park or relocate with a Tenant Protection Voucher to a unit of their choosing.

details.relocationStatus: Incomplete — no comparable housing, no relocation expenses, no counseling

authorities.label: ORCP 29A — Compulsory Joinder (Oregon)
authorities.excerptText: A person who is subject to service of process shall be joined as a party in the action if: (1) in the person's absence complete relief cannot be accorded among those already parties, or (2) the person claims an interest relating to the subject of the action and is so situated that the disposition of the action in the person's absence may as a practical matter impair or impede the person's ability to protect that interest or leave any of the persons already parties subject to a substantial risk of incurring double, multiple, or otherwise inconsistent obligations.
authorities.proposition: Joinder is required where complete relief cannot be accorded without the absent party, or where absence creates risk of inconsistent obligations.

authorities.label: ORCP 22B — Third-Party Practice (Oregon)
authorities.excerptText: A defending party, as a third-party plaintiff, may cause a summons and complaint to be served upon a person not a party to the action who is or may be liable to the third-party plaintiff for all or part of the plaintiff's claim against the third-party plaintiff.
authorities.proposition: A defendant may implead any person who may be liable for all or part of the claim against the defendant.

authorities.label: 42 U.S.C. § 1437p — Section 18 Demolition and Disposition
authorities.excerptText: A public housing agency shall not proceed with the demolition or disposition of any public housing project unless the Secretary has approved the demolition or disposition plan ... [including] resident relocation, 90-day notice, comparable housing, relocation expenses, and consultation requirements.
authorities.proposition: PHAs must satisfy mandatory relocation, notice, comparability, and consultation preconditions before Section 18 demolition or disposition.

authorities.label: 24 CFR 970.7 — Demolition/Disposition Application Requirements
authorities.excerptText: The PHA must submit an application to HUD that includes a relocation plan, consultation records, board resolution, environmental review, and all required supporting documentation before HUD approval may be granted.
authorities.proposition: Full application package including relocation plan and consultation records must precede HUD approval.

authorities.label: 24 CFR 970.21 — Relocation Requirements
authorities.excerptText: The PHA must offer each family a comparable dwelling unit ... [that is] decent, safe, sanitary, and in an area not generally less desirable ... No family may be required to move until a comparable dwelling unit is actually available ... The PHA must pay actual and reasonable moving expenses.
authorities.proposition: Relocation housing must be comparable (HQS-compliant, accessible, similarly desirable). No displacement before relocation is complete. Moving expenses must be paid.

authorities.label: HUD Notice PIH 2019-23 — RAD Tenant Protections and Conversion Requirements
authorities.excerptText: Residents have the right to return to the converted project upon completion. The converting PHA must maintain a relocation plan approved by HUD. The successor owner-operator is bound by all RAD requirements, including tenant protection, lease continuation, and right to return. All tenant rights that existed under the ACC continue under the RAD HAP contract.
authorities.proposition: The RAD successor LP and its property manager inherit the converting PHA's tenant-protection and relocation obligations; right to return is mandatory.

authorities.label: 24 CFR Part 8 — Nondiscrimination Based on Handicap in HUD Programs
authorities.excerptText: Recipients must make reasonable accommodations in rules, policies, practices, or services when necessary to afford a qualified person with a disability equal opportunity to use and enjoy a dwelling.
authorities.proposition: HUD program recipients (including PHAs and their contractors) must provide reasonable accommodations to persons with disabilities.

authorities.label: Fair Housing Act — 42 U.S.C. § 3604(f)
authorities.excerptText: It shall be unlawful to refuse to make reasonable accommodations in rules, policies, practices, or services, when such accommodations may be necessary to afford such person equal opportunity to use and enjoy a dwelling.
authorities.proposition: Property owners and public housing actors must make reasonable accommodations for disability-related housing needs.

authorities.label: Giebeler v. M&B Associates
authorities.excerptText: Imposition of burdensome policies can interfere with disabled persons' right to use and enjoyment of their dwellings.
authorities.proposition: Burdensome housing policies that impair disabled persons' use and enjoyment may require accommodation regardless of standard policy.

authorities.label: ORS 105.135 — Affirmative Defense to Eviction
authorities.excerptText: In any action for possession of a dwelling unit, it is an affirmative defense that the landlord has not complied with applicable federal law governing the displacement of public-housing residents.
authorities.proposition: Non-compliance with Section 18 federal relocation requirements is an affirmative defense to eviction under Oregon law.

authorities.label: HACC Administrative Plan (Chapter 18 — RAD PBV Program)
authorities.pincite: Exhibit 18-1, Hillside Manor Apartments RAD PBV entry
authorities.excerptText: Development Name: Hillside Manor Apartments / Owner Information: Hillside Manor Limited Partnership / Property Management Company: Quantum Residential Property Management (HillsideManor@QResInc.com) / PHA-Owned: Yes / Mixed-Finance Development: Yes / Closing Date: 1/1/2021 / RAD Notice: PIH 2019-23 / HAP Contracts: 06/01/2020 (Eminent Domain 5 PBV plus 26 Enhanced PBV) and 01/01/2021 (RAD HAP contract, 70 RAD PBV).
authorities.proposition: HACC's own filed Administrative Plan names Quantum Residential as property manager of the Hillside Manor Limited Partnership, the private RAD successor entity created from former Hillside Park public housing through eminent domain acquisition and RAD conversion.

notes: Evidence knowledge graph for the Motion for Joinder of Quantum Residential in HACC v. Barber/Cortez, Clackamas County Circuit Court. All evidence nodes reference specific files, exhibit labels, and source lines in the repository. The Admin Plan entries at lines 32152 (Clayton Mohr), 32317 (Hillside Manor Ch17), 33349 (Good Shepherd), and 37308 (Hillside Manor Ch18 RAD) are the four primary authority sources for the HACC-Quantum contractual relationship. The Ferron emails (Exhibit L) are the primary authority for same-transaction participation and quantum's intake-processing role.
